name: copilot-resurrect
description: Automatically resurrects a dead GitHub Copilot Chat session by re-injecting your configured ignition prompt — keep autonomous coding loops alive overnight.

copilot-resurrect.enabled.description: Workspace-only: enable/disable the Copilot session resurrection watcher for the current workspace window.

copilot-resurrect.ignitionPrompt.description: Workspace-only: the prompt to inject when resurrecting a dead Copilot Chat session in this workspace.

copilot-resurrect.silenceTimeoutSeconds.description: Workspace-only: how many seconds of silence before declaring the Copilot Chat session dead and triggering resurrection. Default: 180s (3 minutes). Supported range: 60-1200s.

copilot-resurrect.maxRestartsPerDay.description: Workspace-only: maximum number of automatic session resurrections allowed per calendar day. Prevents infinite-loop storms. Default: 50.

copilot-resurrect.preferredModel.description: Workspace-only: preferred Copilot Chat model for this workspace. The active chat UI model may still need to be selected manually depending on mode limitations.

copilot-resurrect.fallbackModel.description: Workspace-only: legacy single fallback model to suggest when the primary is rate-limited. Prefer fallbackModelChain for new configurations.

copilot-resurrect.fallbackModelChain.description: Workspace-only: ordered list of fallback models to suggest after a rate-limit event. The legacy single fallbackModel setting is still supported and is appended automatically if not already included.

copilot-resurrect.chatParticipant.description: Workspace-only: chat participant to prefix the ignition prompt with (e.g. 'copilot', 'workspace'). Leave blank for no prefix.

copilot-resurrect.agentMode.description: Workspace-only: agent mode to activate when resurrecting a session. Discovers custom agents from .github/agents/*.agent.md in the workspace.

enumDescriptions: Bypass Approvals — skip confirmation prompts (requires manual enable in new sessions)
Autopilot (Preview) — full autonomous mode (requires manual enable in new sessions)
copilot-resurrect.approvalsMode.description: Workspace-only: preferred approvals mode for resurrected sessions. Non-default modes will show a reminder to set this in the Copilot Chat UI when starting new sessions.

copilot-resurrect.rateLimitCooldownBaseS.description: Workspace-only: base cooldown seconds for rate-limit backoff. Each consecutive rate-limit doubles this (exponential backoff). Default: 30s.

copilot-resurrect.rateLimitCooldownMaxSe.description: Workspace-only: maximum cooldown seconds for exponential backoff cap. Default: 600s (10 minutes).

copilot-resurrect.startNewSession.description: Workspace-only: start a new chat session when resurrecting from silence (vs. retrying in the existing session).

copilot-resurrect.preferNewSessionOnRate.description: Workspace-only: when a rate-limit or shadow-rate-limit error is detected, prefer starting a fresh chat session instead of reusing the existing session context.

copilot-resurrect.promptCompactionEnable.description: Workspace-only: compact the ignition prompt for rate-limit recoveries to reduce token pressure.

enumDescriptions: Normalize whitespace and collapse repeated blank lines
Trim obvious prompt bloat while preserving line structure
Rewrite the prompt into a shorter recovery-oriented template
copilot-resurrect.promptCompactionStrate.description: Workspace-only: how the ignition prompt should be compacted during rate-limit recovery when promptCompactionEnabled is turned on.

copilot-resurrect.contentCheckEnabled.description: Workspace-only: enable content-based error detection. Reads Copilot Chat session files to detect rate-limit and server error messages.

copilot-resurrect.watchPaths.description: Workspace-only: override the list of file-system paths the watcher monitors for Copilot Chat activity. Leave empty for automatic discovery (recommended).

copilot-resurrect.watchIgnorePatterns.description: Workspace-only: glob patterns for paths to exclude from workspace activity detection. Matching is performed against both absolute and workspace-relative paths. Useful for silencing noise from .git internals, node_modules, or other non-Copilot file changes. Supports ** (any depth), * (within segment), and ? (single char). Default: ['**/.git/**', '.git/**'].
